title: NIS2 SCADA System Access Monitoring

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (Splunkbase 2891)
spl:
(index=win_sec EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=10 OR Logon_Type=2) OR sourcetype="cyberark:session" earliest=-7d
| eval host=coalesce(dest_nt_host, ComputerName, target_host)
| lookup nis2_scada_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT scada_role plant_site
| where scada_role IN ("HMI","ENG_WORKSTATION","SCADA_SERVER")
| join host [
    | inputlookup nis2_scada_maintenance_windows.csv
    | eval starttime=strptime(start,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
    | eval endtime=strptime(end,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
]
| where _time < starttime OR _time > endtime
| stats values(user) as users count by host plant_site
| sort - count
value: Interactive access to SCADA/HMI layers is high risk. This use case monitors engineering workstation logons, HMI tag writes, and remote-desktop sessions to SCADA hosts — correlating with maintenance windows and approved technician identities.
implementation: (1) Inventory SCADA hosts with roles in `nis2_scada_asset_inventory.csv`; (2) ingest maintenance windows from plant maintenance system; (3) alert on interactive access outside windows; (4) integrate PAM session video references for investigations; (5) align with safety permit-to-work where applicable.
visualization: Table (out-of-window access), Timeline (sessions), Single value (distinct technicians), Map (plant sites).
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
